Q: Charlie has 10 books and gives away 8. How many books does Charlie have left?
A: Charlie has 10-8=2 books left.

Q: Emma shares 32 coins equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 32/2=16 coins.

Q: David has 34 apples and gets 45 more. How many apples does David have?
A: David has 34+45=79 apples.

Q: Frank buys 5 boxes with 11 oranges each. How many oranges in total?
A: Frank has 5*11=55 oranges in total.

A: Frank has 23+44=67 stickers.

Q: Frank buys 17 boxes with 13 apples each. How many apples in total?
A: Frank has 17*13=221 apples in total.

Q: Jack shares 5 oranges equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 5/5=1 oranges.

A: Each friend gets 10/5=2 apples.

Q: Emma has 40 coins and gives away 28. How many coins does Emma have left?
A: Emma has 40-28=12 coins left.

Q: Frank buys 8 boxes with 4 cookies each. How many cookies in total?
A: Frank has 8*4=32 cookies in total.

Q: Bob has 27 marbles and gives away 4. How many marbles does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 27-4=23 marbles left.

Q: Frank has 21 flowers and gets 3 more. How many flowers does Frank have?
A: Frank has 21+3=24 flowers.

Q: Alice has 57 flowers and gives away 11. How many flowers does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 57-11=46 flowers left.

A: Bob has 30-15=15 candies left.

A: Each friend gets 36/4=9 flowers.

Q: Harry has 58 oranges and gives away 6. How many oranges does Harry have left?
A: Harry has 58-6=52 oranges left.

Q: Emma has 27 oranges and gets 32 more. How many oranges does Emma have?
A: Emma has 27+32=59 oranges.

Q: Grace has 92 flowers and gives away 49. How many flowers does Grace have left?
A: Grace has 92-49=43 flowers left.

Q: Harry has 45 books and gets 47 more. How many books does Harry have?
A: Harry has 45+47=92 books.

Q: Grace buys 5 boxes with 12 cookies each. How many cookies in total?
A: Grace has 5*12=60 cookies in total.

Q: Charlie has 5 marbles and gets 10 more. How many marbles does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 5+10=15 marbles.

Q: Bob has 45 pencils and gives away 21. How many pencils does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 45-21=24 pencils left.

A: Bob has 2*5=10 marbles in total.

A: Harry has 2*3=6 marbles in total.

A: Grace has 37-34=3 cookies left.

A: Each friend gets 6/3=2 cookies.

A: Grace has 5+6=11 coins.

Q: Jack buys 11 boxes with 19 coins each. How many coins in total?
A: Jack has 11*19=209 coins in total.

Q: Harry buys 17 boxes with 7 flowers each. How many flowers in total?
A: Harry has 17*7=119 flowers in total.

Q: Ivy buys 4 boxes with 7 marbles each. How many marbles in total?
A: Ivy has 4*7=28 marbles in total.

Q: Bob buys 8 boxes with 10 cookies each. How many cookies in total?
A: Bob has 8*10=80 cookies in total.

Q: Grace has 27 candies and gives away 13. How many candies does Grace have left?
A: Grace has 27-13=14 candies left.

Q: Alice has 13 flowers and gets 7 more. How many flowers does Alice have?
A: Alice has 13+7=20 flowers.

Q: Ivy buys 20 boxes with 2 coins each. How many coins in total?
A: Ivy has 20*2=40 coins in total.

Q: Harry has 35 pencils and gets 31 more. How many pencils does Harry have?
A: Harry has 35+31=66 pencils.

A: Jack has 10*3=30 books in total.

Q: Charlie shares 108 apples equally among 9 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 108/9=12 apples.

Q: Bob has 37 coins and gives away 24. How many coins does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 37-24=13 coins left.

Q: Alice buys 16 boxes with 8 candies each. How many candies in total?
A: Alice has 16*8=128 candies in total.

A: Each friend gets 135/9=15 marbles.

Q: Grace shares 85 books equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 85/5=17 books.

Q: Ivy has 90 pencils and gives away 74. How many pencils does Ivy have left?
A: Ivy has 90-74=16 pencils left.

Q: Bob has 30 books and gives away 11. How many books does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 30-11=19 books left.

Q: David has 82 candies and gives away 61. How many candies does David have left?
A: David has 82-61=21 candies left.

Q: Bob has 45 marbles and gets 6 more. How many marbles does Bob have?
A: Bob has 45+6=51 marbles.

Q: Harry buys 6 boxes with 1 oranges each. How many oranges in total?
A: Harry has 6*1=6 oranges in total.

Q: Harry shares 140 apples equally among 10 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 140/10=14 apples.

Q: Bob buys 5 boxes with 17 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: Bob has 5*17=85 pencils in total.

Q: Bob has 28 oranges and gives away 17. How many oranges does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 28-17=11 oranges left.

A: Grace has 3+46=49 pencils.

Q: Jack shares 72 coins equally among 9 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 72/9=8 coins.

Q: Frank buys 18 boxes with 12 candies each. How many candies in total?
A: Frank has 18*12=216 candies in total.

Q: Frank has 30 stickers and gives away 27. How many stickers does Frank have left?
A: Frank has 30-27=3 stickers left.

A: Jack has 14*8=112 marbles in total.

Q: Frank has 19 stickers and gets 43 more. How many stickers does Frank have?
A: Frank has 19+43=62 stickers.

Q: Harry has 56 oranges and gives away 28. How many oranges does Harry have left?
A: Harry has 56-28=28 oranges left.

Q: Harry has 28 books and gets 15 more. How many books does Harry have?
A: Harry has 28+15=43 books.

A: Grace has 15-15=0 oranges left.

A: Bob has 47+33=80 stickers.

A: Each friend gets 51/3=17 candies.

A: Bob has 6*10=60 apples in total.

A: Harry has 80-38=42 cookies left.

Q: Alice has 34 stickers and gives away 11. How many stickers does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 34-11=23 stickers left.

Q: Frank shares 135 pencils equally among 9 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 135/9=15 pencils.

Q: Bob shares 28 stickers equally among 7 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 28/7=4 stickers.

Q: Alice has 14 oranges and gets 5 more. How many oranges does Alice have?
A: Alice has 14+5=19 oranges.

Q: Grace shares 95 oranges equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 95/5=19 oranges.

Q: Charlie has 50 coins and gets 11 more. How many coins does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 50+11=61 coins.

Q: David has 81 stickers and gives away 66. How many stickers does David have left?
A: David has 81-66=15 stickers left.

Q: Emma shares 48 cookies equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 48/3=16 cookies.

A: Emma has 20*11=220 oranges in total.

A: Harry has 10*17=170 marbles in total.

A: Charlie has 13*4=52 pencils in total.

Q: David has 37 oranges and gets 45 more. How many oranges does David have?
A: David has 37+45=82 oranges.

A: Charlie has 34+28=62 pencils.

A: David has 23+42=65 books.

A: Frank has 50+18=68 cookies.

Q: Bob buys 7 boxes with 19 flowers each. How many flowers in total?
A: Bob has 7*19=133 flowers in total.

Q: Jack buys 11 boxes with 8 oranges each. How many oranges in total?
A: Jack has 11*8=88 oranges in total.

Q: Ivy has 20 apples and gets 45 more. How many apples does Ivy have?
A: Ivy has 20+45=65 apples.

Q: Emma has 73 stickers and gives away 9. How many stickers does Emma have left?
A: Emma has 73-9=64 stickers left.

A: Each friend gets 32/2=16 cookies.

Q: Alice shares 28 pencils equally among 7 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 28/7=4 pencils.

Q: Emma shares 40 candies equally among 4 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 40/4=10 candies.

Q: Bob has 74 apples and gives away 64. How many apples does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 74-64=10 apples left.

Q: Frank has 41 flowers and gets 34 more. How many flowers does Frank have?
A: Frank has 41+34=75 flowers.

Q: Ivy has 27 candies and gives away 22. How many candies does Ivy have left?
A: Ivy has 27-22=5 candies left.

Q: Alice has 43 coins and gives away 40. How many coins does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 43-40=3 coins left.

Q: Emma buys 15 boxes with 14 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: Emma has 15*14=210 pencils in total.

Q: Bob has 26 oranges and gets 23 more. How many oranges does Bob have?
A: Bob has 26+23=49 oranges.

Q: Bob shares 22 coins equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 22/2=11 coins.

Q: Charlie has 26 flowers and gets 35 more. How many flowers does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 26+35=61 flowers.

Q: Bob has 38 coins and gets 32 more. How many coins does Bob have?
A: Bob has 38+32=70 coins.

A: Ivy has 25-20=5 candies left.

A: Each friend gets 28/2=14 flowers.

A: Grace has 12*16=192 candies in total.

A: David has 19*1=19 marbles in total.

A: Ivy has 24+6=30 oranges.

Q: Bob has 71 pencils and gives away 41. How many pencils does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 71-41=30 pencils left.

Q: Charlie has 45 coins and gives away 5. How many coins does Charlie have left?
A: Charlie has 45-5=40 coins left.

Q: Charlie has 43 oranges and gets 40 more. How many oranges does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 43+40=83 oranges.

Q: David buys 8 boxes with 4 cookies each. How many cookies in total?
A: David has 8*4=32 cookies in total.

A: Each friend gets 120/6=20 cookies.

A: Each friend gets 49/7=7 books.

Q: Ivy shares 60 candies equally among 10 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 60/10=6 candies.

Q: Emma has 42 books and gets 36 more. How many books does Emma have?
A: Emma has 42+36=78 books.

Q: Charlie has 24 pencils and gives away 18. How many pencils does Charlie have left?
A: Charlie has 24-18=6 pencils left.

A: Emma has 9*4=36 flowers in total.

Q: Harry has 18 stickers and gets 30 more. How many stickers does Harry have?
A: Harry has 18+30=48 stickers.

Q: Grace has 8 candies and gets 44 more. How many candies does Grace have?
A: Grace has 8+44=52 candies.

Q: Charlie buys 3 boxes with 15 apples each. How many apples in total?
A: Charlie has 3*15=45 apples in total.

Q: Frank has 5 oranges and gets 33 more. How many oranges does Frank have?
A: Frank has 5+33=38 oranges.

Q: Bob shares 45 marbles equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 45/3=15 marbles.

Q: Alice has 53 oranges and gives away 47. How many oranges does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 53-47=6 oranges left.

A: Bob has 16*12=192 flowers in total.

Q: Frank has 12 apples and gets 25 more. How many apples does Frank have?
A: Frank has 12+25=37 apples.

A: Charlie has 18*3=54 pencils in total.

Q: Frank has 75 oranges and gives away 54. How many oranges does Frank have left?
A: Frank has 75-54=21 oranges left.

Q: Jack buys 7 boxes with 10 flowers each. How many flowers in total?
A: Jack has 7*10=70 flowers in total.

Q: Alice has 10 pencils and gives away 1. How many pencils does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 10-1=9 pencils left.

Q: Grace shares 6 cookies equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 6/2=3 cookies.

Q: Jack has 97 cookies and gives away 50. How many cookies does Jack have left?
A: Jack has 97-50=47 cookies left.

Q: Alice has 19 books and gets 10 more. How many books does Alice have?
A: Alice has 19+10=29 books.

Q: Charlie shares 102 candies equally among 6 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 102/6=17 candies.

Q: Bob has 7 coins and gets 7 more. How many coins does Bob have?
A: Bob has 7+7=14 coins.

A: Alice has 19*20=380 coins in total.

A: Ivy has 29+25=54 pencils.

Q: Bob has 44 oranges and gets 45 more. How many oranges does Bob have?
A: Bob has 44+45=89 oranges.

Q: Charlie buys 11 boxes with 8 books each. How many books in total?
A: Charlie has 11*8=88 books in total.

Q: Alice buys 10 boxes with 9 stickers each. How many stickers in total?
A: Alice has 10*9=90 stickers in total.

Q: Grace shares 140 candies equally among 10 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 140/10=14 candies.

Q: Emma shares 120 marbles equally among 6 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 120/6=20 marbles.

A: Emma has 14*5=70 cookies in total.

A: Bob has 14+34=48 oranges.

A: Each friend gets 22/2=11 books.

A: Bob has 31+5=36 marbles.

A: Alice has 11-1=10 stickers left.

Q: Ivy shares 25 stickers equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 25/5=5 stickers.

Q: Emma has 89 marbles and gives away 12. How many marbles does Emma have left?
A: Emma has 89-12=77 marbles left.

Q: David buys 9 boxes with 11 oranges each. How many oranges in total?
A: David has 9*11=99 oranges in total.

A: Frank has 42+50=92 stickers.

A: Bob has 46+43=89 apples.

Q: Bob buys 16 boxes with 7 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: Bob has 16*7=112 pencils in total.

Q: David has 51 candies and gives away 7. How many candies does David have left?
A: David has 51-7=44 candies left.

Q: Grace buys 16 boxes with 19 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: Grace has 16*19=304 pencils in total.

Q: Jack has 70 cookies and gives away 8. How many cookies does Jack have left?
A: Jack has 70-8=62 cookies left.

Q: Emma has 19 stickers and gets 10 more. How many stickers does Emma have?
A: Emma has 19+10=29 stickers.

A: Each friend gets 51/3=17 marbles.

A: Bob has 60-50=10 oranges left.

Q: Harry shares 18 coins equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 18/3=6 coins.

Q: Frank shares 36 cookies equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 36/2=18 cookies.

Q: Bob has 16 cookies and gives away 13. How many cookies does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 16-13=3 cookies left.

Q: Harry buys 8 boxes with 15 books each. How many books in total?
A: Harry has 8*15=120 books in total.

Q: Harry has 30 books and gives away 26. How many books does Harry have left?
A: Harry has 30-26=4 books left.

Q: Charlie has 48 pencils and gets 19 more. How many pencils does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 48+19=67 pencils.

A: Frank has 16-16=0 cookies left.

A: Each friend gets 24/8=3 books.

A: Grace has 12*20=240 coins in total.

A: Ivy has 35+24=59 flowers.

A: Frank has 36-35=1 apples left.

Q: Grace shares 80 stickers equally among 8 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 80/8=10 stickers.

A: Alice has 8*3=24 coins in total.

Q: Harry shares 48 oranges equally among 4 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 48/4=12 oranges.

Q: Jack shares 20 pencils equally among 10 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 20/10=2 pencils.

Q: Frank shares 65 flowers equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 65/5=13 flowers.

Q: Charlie shares 15 flowers equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 15/3=5 flowers.

Q: Bob has 29 apples and gives away 1. How many apples does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 29-1=28 apples left.

A: Bob has 9*18=162 marbles in total.

Q: Bob has 23 pencils and gets 36 more. How many pencils does Bob have?
A: Bob has 23+36=59 pencils.

A: Harry has 7*17=119 apples in total.

Q: Grace shares 6 coins equally among 6 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 6/6=1 coins.

Q: David shares 104 books equally among 8 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 104/8=13 books.

Q: Harry buys 19 boxes with 18 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: Harry has 19*18=342 pencils in total.

Q: Grace has 43 oranges and gives away 9. How many oranges does Grace have left?
A: Grace has 43-9=34 oranges left.

Q: David buys 14 boxes with 19 pencils each. How many pencils in total?
A: David has 14*19=266 pencils in total.

Q: David has 6 flowers and gets 28 more. How many flowers does David have?
A: David has 6+28=34 flowers.

A: Each friend gets 133/7=19 cookies.

Q: Emma has 80 cookies and gives away 33. How many cookies does Emma have left?
A: Emma has 80-33=47 cookies left.

Q: Emma shares 28 books equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 28/2=14 books.

Q: Harry shares 144 pencils equally among 9 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 144/9=16 pencils.

A: Jack has 13*17=221 apples in total.

Q: Harry has 46 oranges and gets 40 more. How many oranges does Harry have?
A: Harry has 46+40=86 oranges.

Q: Frank shares 24 cookies equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 24/2=12 cookies.

Q: Alice has 23 apples and gives away 6. How many apples does Alice have left?
A: Alice has 23-6=17 apples left.

Q: Emma shares 16 marbles equally among 4 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 16/4=4 marbles.

Q: Harry has 32 marbles and gives away 30. How many marbles does Harry have left?
A: Harry has 32-30=2 marbles left.

Q: Charlie shares 18 flowers equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 18/3=6 flowers.

Q: Charlie buys 9 boxes with 4 marbles each. How many marbles in total?
A: Charlie has 9*4=36 marbles in total.

A: Each friend gets 45/9=5 apples.

A: Ivy has 11*9=99 candies in total.

A: Frank has 8*15=120 coins in total.

A: Frank has 63-31=32 candies left.

Q: Emma has 64 stickers and gives away 52. How many stickers does Emma have left?
A: Emma has 64-52=12 stickers left.

Q: Charlie has 29 marbles and gives away 25. How many marbles does Charlie have left?
A: Charlie has 29-25=4 marbles left.

A: Bob has 7+21=28 oranges.

Q: Harry shares 2 marbles equally among 2 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 2/2=1 marbles.

Q: Harry shares 32 coins equally among 4 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 32/4=8 coins.

Q: Emma buys 20 boxes with 1 candies each. How many candies in total?
A: Emma has 20*1=20 candies in total.

Q: Ivy shares 112 apples equally among 8 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 112/8=14 apples.

Q: Charlie has 23 stickers and gets 1 more. How many stickers does Charlie have?
A: Charlie has 23+1=24 stickers.

A: Each friend gets 54/6=9 apples.

A: Frank has 17-15=2 oranges left.

A: Each friend gets 65/5=13 oranges.

Q: Ivy shares 70 apples equally among 5 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 70/5=14 apples.

Q: Alice has 50 cookies and gets 42 more. How many cookies does Alice have?
A: Alice has 50+42=92 cookies.

Q: Harry shares 102 coins equally among 6 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 102/6=17 coins.

Q: Grace shares 3 flowers equally among 3 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 3/3=1 flowers.

Q: Frank has 9 cookies and gets 50 more. How many cookies does Frank have?
A: Frank has 9+50=59 cookies.

Q: Bob has 99 flowers and gives away 23. How many flowers does Bob have left?
A: Bob has 99-23=76 flowers left.

Q: Emma shares 40 stickers equally among 4 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 40/4=10 stickers.

Q: Harry has 27 marbles and gets 30 more. How many marbles does Harry have?
A: Harry has 27+30=57 marbles.

A: Emma has 98-29=69 books left.

Q: Bob buys 2 boxes with 4 flowers each. How many flowers in total?
A: Bob has 2*4=8 flowers in total.

Q: David has 10 stickers and gets 47 more. How many stickers does David have?
A: David has 10+47=57 stickers.

Q: Harry has 36 oranges and gets 23 more. How many oranges does Harry have?
A: Harry has 36+23=59 oranges.

Q: Grace shares 42 cookies equally among 6 friends. How many does each get?
A: Each friend gets 42/6=7 cookies.

A: Jack has 25+49=74 books.

A: Frank has 38+30=68 oranges.

A: Each friend gets 108/9=12 apples.

A: Emma has 16+32=48 apples.

A: Harry has 19-13=6 flowers left.

Q: Ivy has 83 flowers and gives away 23. How many flowers does Ivy have left?
A: Ivy has 83-23=60 flowers left.

A: Alice has 44+33=77 pencils.
